goal: Intent B: Packages metric (abstractness + resolve_packages + Structure.packages); rewire architecture.py; retire robert.py

summary.title: Packages metric on Structure view; retire robert.py (Intent B)
summary.what: Adds Language.is_abstract_scope + Language.resolve_packages classmethods with per-grammar overrides for Python (ABC/Protocol/ABCMeta), Java/C#/TS (abstract modifier + interface), Rust (trait), Go (interface_type/struct_type via type_spec descent), Ruby (module abstract, class concrete), and JS (all classes concrete). Adds Structure.packages() view method backed by slop.structure._packages helper plus PackageMetrics frozen record. Rewires structural.packages.rigidity and .uselessness to consume Structure (drops legacy_v2_shim wrapper on both rules; both rule signatures change to (structure, rc, sc)). Filters Ruby's open-class .<anonymous> aggregation duplicates so Na/Nc don't double. Deletes slop._structural.robert (1087 lines). 18 new/rewritten tests + 5 new Structure.packages unit tests; full suite 532/532.
summary.why: structural.packages.rigidity/uselessness were pure passthroughs to a 1087-line pre-substrate kernel (robert_kernel) that duplicated file discovery, language resolution, package grouping, abstractness classification, and import-graph aggregation. A line-for-line port would have re-imported logic that now belongs on Language and Structure. The user explicitly chose redesign over retrofit: the Robert metrics were the seed of slop (written before the unified linter existed) and needed to be redrawn as a v2.0 feature, with the legacy kernel as a behavioural reference only. Intent B closes the loop on a multi-intent rework that started with Intent A (import graph).
summary.user_goal: Intent B: Packages metric (abstractness + resolve_packages + Structure.packages); rewire architecture.py; retire robert.py

decisions.rationale: Single per-grammar classmethod that returns True for abstract types (Na), False for concrete (Nc), None for uncountable (skip). Cleaner than parallel abstract_scope_types / concrete_scope_types frozensets when the classification needs a modifier check (Java/C#/TS abstract keyword, Python superclass inspection, Go type_spec descent). The None case handles partial-coverage languages (Rust enums uncovered, C/C++ uncovered) honestly — they end up in 'unknown' zone instead of being falsely tagged Pain.

Legacy kernel treated languages without abstractness classification (C, C++ above threshold-fail of pure-virtual detection) as Na=0, Nc=N → A=0 → universally Pain. That's a false signal: it claims rigidity for files where the tool has no abstractness evidence at all. New view emits zone='unknown' when Na+Nc=0 (no scopes were classified). Rules silently skip unknown packages; they neither pass nor fail. Tradeoff: fewer false positives, fewer false negatives, but a quieter rule for grammars that haven't been built out yet.

Ruby's open-class aggregation (recent CK follow-up) emits a synthetic anonymous child scope alongside every real class/module scope. Without filtering, Na and Nc each double. The filter sits in _packages.py rather than Language.is_abstract_scope because the duplicate is a tree-walker artifact, not a per-language abstractness call.

Legacy hardcoded an allowlist (go, python, java, c_sharp, typescript, javascript, rust) and returned status=skip for anything outside. With per-grammar opt-in via is_abstract_scope, that filter is redundant — grammars without overrides naturally produce zone='unknown' and contribute zero violations. The skip-when-no-supported-languages tests were removed; the rule now reports on every package the substrate produces.

decisions.chose: Architecture rule signature: (structure, rc, sc) — drops shim
decisions.rationale: Direct view-native dispatch through _execute_rule_v2. No more legacy_v2_shim wrapper. Two of the 12 remaining shimmed rules retire; 10 still wrapped (cycles, sibling_calls, stringly_typed, out_parameters, any_type_density, clone_density, and the 7 lexical rules).

review_notes: Parity vs legacy robert_kernel on slop source (22 Python packages): Na/Nc identical across all packages — abstractness classification is exactly correct. Ca/Ce slightly higher in the new graph (matches Intent A's improved __init__.py re-export resolution). Zone shifts in 6 packages all trace to improved import resolution, not to logic divergence.

Rule-layer tests use monkeypatch on Structure.packages for zone-isolation tests (cleaner than constructing fixtures whose Ca/Ce/Na/Nc happen to land in the right quadrant). Per-language abstractness tests use real Tree-built fixtures.
deps_kernel still alive — structural.deps cycle rule still consumes it. Intent C (follow-up) will port cycles onto Structure.dependency_graph and retire deps.py.
Ruby module-classification test in test_rules/test_ruby.py was migrated from kernel-direct to view-path. Existing CK follow-up tests untouched.

architectural_claims: Packages metric is substrate-aligned: per-grammar abstractness classification (Language.is_abstract_scope) + per-grammar package resolution (Language.resolve_packages) + view-method compute (Structure.packages).
robert_kernel retired. structural.packages.rigidity and .uselessness no longer rely on any legacy kernel; their substrate is end-to-end v2.0.
'unknown' zone is the honest classification for languages without abstractness signal, replacing legacy's universal-Pain fallback for the same situation.
behavioral_changes: structural.packages.rigidity / .uselessness signature: (structure, rule_config, slop_config) instead of (root, rule_config, slop_config). All downstream dispatch already passes Structure when no shim wraps the rule.
Packages with zero coupling data (no inter-package imports) now classify as 'unknown' instead of being computed against legacy fallbacks.
Rule no longer skips when no supported languages — it processes every grammar that has classification opinions.
migration_notes: Custom rules that imported from slop._structural.robert will break. The replacement is slop.structure.records.PackageMetrics and slop.structure.view.Structure.packages.

Custom rules that called legacy_v2_shim(run_rigidity) directly will fail — run_rigidity now takes Structure first. The standard rule registry path (structural.rules.STRUCTURAL_RULES) is the supported interface and was migrated in this commit.
